skill.name: internal-comms
skill.description: Helps draft internal communications using structured formats for 3P updates, newsletters, FAQs, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.
skill.summary: Drafts clear internal updates using reusable company communication formats.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Official Anthropic skill with markdown guidance and example formats only. Static indicators relate to optional use of workplace sources such as Slack, email, calendar, documents, and press references. No executable code, destructive behavior, credential handling, or prompt injection attempts were found.

content.value_statement: Internal updates can become inconsistent when each team invents a new format. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code draft clear messages using proven company communication patterns.
actual_capabilities: Drafts 3P updates with Progress, Plans, and Problems sections.
Creates company newsletter outlines with grouped bullet sections.
Writes FAQ answers based on broad employee questions.
Adapts general internal messages by audience, purpose, tone, and format.
Requests missing context when team, audience, time period, or source details are unclear.
Uses concise language and links when source material is available.
limitations: Requires user-provided context or approved tool access to include current company facts.
Does not verify sensitive announcements without authoritative source material.
Does not replace legal, HR, security, or executive review for high-impact messages.
Provides communication structure, not a complete internal approval workflow.

use_cases.title: Weekly Team Update
use_cases.description: Turn team accomplishments, next steps, and blockers into a concise 3P update for leaders.

use_cases.title: Company Newsletter
use_cases.description: Summarize major announcements, milestones, and leadership posts for a broad employee audience.

use_cases.title: Employee FAQ
use_cases.description: Convert repeated employee questions into direct answers with links to trusted company sources.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a 3P Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with direct source notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a 3P update for [team] covering [dates]. Use these notes for Progress, Plans, and Problems: [notes]. Keep it brief.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Newsletter Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a company newsletter section for [audience] about [topic]. Group related items and keep each bullet under two sentences.

prompt_templates.title: Answer Common Employee Questions
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft FAQ answers for these employee questions: [questions]. Use only the provided source notes and flag anything that needs official confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize Internal Sources
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with multiple source types.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Slack, email, document, and calendar notes: [source excerpts]. Produce an internal update for [audience] with links, priorities, risks, and open questions.

output_examples.input: Create a weekly 3P update for the Data Platform team from these launch, hiring, and blocker notes.
output_examples.output: Data Platform, June 24 to June 30. Progress covers launch completion and hiring progress. Plans cover migration work. Problems cover unresolved data quality blockers.

output_examples.input: Summarize this month for a company newsletter using all-hands notes, executive emails, and launch announcements.
output: Company Announcements: concise bullets on leadership messages and major policy changes.
Progress on Priorities: grouped updates for product, customers, and operations.
Social Updates: broad employee moments and links to source posts.

output_examples.input: Draft FAQ answers for employee questions about a new planning process.
output_examples.output: Question and answer pairs explain what is changing, when it starts, who owns decisions, and where employees can read the source document.

best_practices: Provide the audience, purpose, source notes, and desired delivery channel before drafting.
Use authoritative company sources for announcements, dates, metrics, and policy details.
Ask for review from the responsible owner before sharing sensitive or company-wide messages.
anti_patterns: Do not invent metrics, decisions, owners, or timelines when source material is missing.
Do not publish sensitive HR, legal, security, or financial updates without approved review.
Do not mix team-specific detail into company-wide newsletters unless it affects most employees.

faq.question: What types of internal communication does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQ answers, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident reports.

faq.question: Does this skill need access to workplace tools?
faq.answer: It works with pasted notes, but approved access to Slack, email, calendar, or documents can improve source coverage.

faq.question: Can it write company-wide announcements?
faq.answer: Yes, it can draft company-wide updates from approved source material and organize them for Slack or email.

faq.question: How does it handle missing context?
faq.answer: It asks for missing details such as audience, team name, time period, tone, and required format.

faq.question: Can it answer sensitive employee questions?
faq.answer: It can draft careful answers from official sources, but sensitive topics should receive owner or executive review.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The official skill contains markdown guidance only and has no executable code or dangerous patterns.
